description: This architecture represents a "Supervised Trading Copilot" designed for high-reliability decision support. The system follows a Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) pattern where data flows from ingestion and market providers through a multi-agent analysis pipeline, culminating in a "Decision Spine" that stages trades for human approval or automated execution. The architecture emphasizes resilience through a dedicated fault-injection layer and maintains a strict separation between live trading state and the research/backtesting sandbox.

components.name: Orchestration & Interface Hub
components.description: Central nervous system managing the Electron-based desktop environment, Express API server, and command routing logic. Bridges human operator interactions with the underlying agentic system.

components.name: System Gateway & UI Shell
components.description: Manages the application's lifecycle, including the Electron desktop window, the Express API server, and the routing of requests to internal agents. It provides the physical interface and the network entry point for all operations.

components.name: Operator Command & Presentation Layer
components.description: Translates internal system states into human-readable formats and processes operator-driven commands. It handles the logic for rendering complex data (like trade confluences or verdicts) into the UI.

components.name: Intelligence & Strategy Engine
components.description: The core analytical component that processes market signals and analyst inputs to derive trade decisions. It maintains "level memory" and applies strategy logic to identify high-probability entry zones.

components.name: Execution & Safety Controller
components.description: Responsible for the final validation and transmission of trades to the broker. It monitors system health to prevent execution during unstable conditions and manages risk parameters.

components.name: Strategy Research & Backtesting Lab
components.description: An offline environment used to replay historical data and validate new state machines or strategies without affecting the live trading environment.

components_relations.relation: Routes incoming operator requests and UI interactions for command processing and data formatting
Requests current market confluences and trade decisions to display to the operator
Passes validated trade setups and strategy signals for final risk checking and broker execution
Reports system health status and execution confirmations back to the main orchestration layer for UI updates
Feeds simulated signals and historical data into the strategy logic to validate performance

components.name: Multi-Agent Intelligence System
components.description: Collection of specialized autonomous agents processing specific domains such as health monitoring, financial forecasting, and workflow automation. Maintains persistent memory and reports structured insights.

components.name: Agent Execution Runtime
components.description: The primary worker layer consisting of specialized autonomous agents (00-14). Each agent manages a specific domain (e.g., Trading, Health, Workflows) and executes logic based on incoming commands or scheduled triggers.

components.name: Cognitive Core & Memory Spines
components.description: The state-aggregation engine that builds a unified Brain snapshot. It transforms raw agent events into structured Spines (Automation, Career, Daily) to provide long-term context and persistent memory across the system.

components.name: Intelligence Gateway
components.description: The external interface for the system's intelligence. It handles human inquiries, routes telemetry requests, and provides status reporting, acting as the bridge between the internal Brain logic and the user/UI.

components.name: Temporal Orchestrator
components.description: Manages the lifecycle of the system through periodic tasks, agent assessments, and heartbeat monitoring. It ensures that the multi-agent system maintains its operational cadence.

components.name: Market & Information Ingestion
components.description: Responsible for fetching external data (RSS, News) and applying analytical models (Sienna Regime) to provide environmental context for the trading agents and daily briefings.

components_relations.relation: Triggers periodic domain-specific tasks and health assessments
Persists event logs and state updates to be synthesized into domain spines
Provides curated news briefs and market regime context for state synthesis
Queries the brain snapshot and spines to answer user inquiries
Schedules the polling of RSS feeds and news updates
Routes direct commands and manual overrides from the user to specific agents

components.name: Signal Ingestion & Market Data
components.description: Gateway for all external data. Parses unstructured signals from Discord and social feeds into structured Levels while providing unified abstraction for real-time and historical price data.

components.name: Ingestion Gateway & Command Router
components.description: Acts as the primary interface for external triggers, routing raw signal data from Discord slash commands or manual inputs to the appropriate provider-specific processing logic.

components.name: Multi-Modal Signal Transformation Engine
components.description: A hybrid parsing engine that combines regex-based text extraction with LLM-powered vision analysis to convert unstructured provider signals into standardized price levels and zones.

components.name: Market Context & Validation Provider
components.description: Provides the real-time price grounding necessary to validate ingested signals, ensuring that parsed levels are within realistic market bounds before they are committed to memory.

components.name: Level Memory & Idempotency Manager
components.description: The final stage of the ingestion pipeline; it ensures data integrity by filtering duplicate signals via source IDs and persisting the structured levels into JSONL-based session memory.

components_relations.relation: Routes raw message payloads and image URLs for structural parsing
Requests current price data to resolve relative terms or validate level sanity
Passes structured level objects for deduplication and long-term storage
Queries market state to resolve conflicts between overlapping historical levels and current price action
calls

components.name: Confluence & Decision Engine
components.description: Core analytical engine synthesizing data from multiple mentors to identify high-probability trade setups. Uses Decision Spine to evaluate confluence across timeframes and strategies.

components.name: Strategy Detection & Confluence Engine
components.description: Identifies high-probability trade setups by merging legacy mentor levels with modern structural analysis. It acts as the primary detector, scanning for 'Fake Breakdown' patterns across multiple timeframes and validating them against trusted external levels.

components.name: Risk Simulation & Capital Allocation
components.description: Normalizes price action across different instruments and simulates trade performance under specific financial constraints. It transforms raw 'setups' into 'executable trades' by calculating position sizing, max heat, and risk-to-reward ratios tailored for proprietary firm account rules.

components.name: Context Synthesis & Attribution
components.description: The final stage of the decision spine that prepares data for the 'Brain' (LLM) and evaluates the accuracy of the engine's logic. It aggregates complex quantitative metrics into natural language context blocks and performs post-hoc attribution to identify which mentors or strategies are currently outperforming.

components_relations.relation: Passes validated trade setups and ranked levels for financial feasibility filtering
Provides simulated performance metrics and risk-adjusted trade parameters for prompt construction
Sends raw detection events and level hits for historical replay and mentor attribution analysis

components.name: Trading Execution & Safety
components.description: Manages trade lifecycle including order reconciliation with Tradovate broker and risk management. Includes Chaos Engineering module for fault injection to ensure graceful recovery.

components.name: Broker Reconciliation & Execution Engine
components.description: The core operational component responsible for synchronizing local trade state with the Tradovate broker. It fetches live positions and orders, reconciles them against internal memory, and manages the transition of trades from staged to executed status.

components.name: Execution Guardrails & Routing
components.description: Acts as the safety boundary manager for the execution engine. It calculates "Avoid Zones" and price boundaries based on market context to prevent orders from being placed in high-risk or invalid areas.

components.name: Chaos Engineering & Resilience Suite
components.description: A specialized testing harness that simulates system failures, such as corrupted snapshots or broker-local state mismatches. It uses a dedicated utility library to manipulate the trading environment and verify that the system's recovery logic functions as expected.

components_relations.relation: Provides price boundaries and Avoid Zones that constrain where the engine is permitted to open or manage positions
Injects synthetic state failures and network errors into the engine to validate its reconciliation and recovery logic
Supplies current position and order status to inform the dynamic calculation of safety boundaries

components.name: Research & Backtesting Sandbox
components.description: Isolated environment for validating strategies against historical data. Simulates trade management and audits system coverage, providing feedback loop for Intelligence System refinement.

components.name: Data Ingestion & Context Engine
components.description: Responsible for the extraction, normalization, and temporal alignment of market data. It transforms unstructured signals from external sources (Discord, text reports) and raw CSV bar data into a unified historical context, including session-based price structures and volatility metrics (ATR).

components.name: Strategy Candidate Generator
components.description: Identifies and clusters price levels to detect high-probability trade setups. It maps historical price action against ingested levels to generate "candidates"—discrete entry opportunities that serve as the input for the simulation engine.

components.name: ATM Execution Simulator
components.description: The core simulation engine that applies Automated Trade Management (ATM) logic to trade candidates. It calculates real-time PnL, manages stop-loss/take-profit adjustments based on bar-by-bar price movement, and generates detailed performance reports in Markdown format.

components.name: Performance Analytics & Audit
components.description: Provides the statistical feedback loop by analyzing simulation outcomes. It audits "coverage" (how many signals were captured) and "alignment" (how signals performed relative to heatmap confluences), producing win-rate metrics that drive system optimization.

components_relations.relation: Supplies normalized price bars and external levels to identify trade setups
Passes validated trade candidates (entry, initial stop, target) for execution simulation
Provides raw trade results and PnL data for statistical auditing and alignment analysis
Informs adjustments to clustering logic and candidate filtering based on historical win-rate performance
